# RoomChe

> RoomChe turns a physical room into a private, queryable database of what you own. You photograph each object, name it, record where it lives, then give it a verdict: keep, hold, or let go. The result is a searchable catalogue of your belongings that stays private to you.

## What it is

- A personal home inventory web app. Every object you own becomes a searchable row.
- Built around photographing, naming, and locating items, then filtering by room, category, or the year an item arrived.
- Each item gets a verdict so the room slowly learns what it is for: kept, uncertain, or to part with.
- Includes duplicate detection so you can spot things you already own before keeping more.

## Who it is for

- People decluttering a room or a whole home who want a record instead of guesswork.
- Anyone who wants a private catalogue of belongings for insurance, moving, or simple recall.

## Access

- The landing page at / is public. Everything else, the catalogue and room views, sits behind login and is private to each user.
- Sign in with a Google account.

## Privacy

- A user's catalogue is theirs alone. Item data is not shared or made public.

## Links

- Home: https://roomche.app/
- Sign in: https://roomche.app/login
